methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The sum of unallocated available rewards and all pending allocated service rewards must not exceed the total contract balance.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the protocol allocates more rewards than it holds in balance, the contract becomes insolvent. Users will be unable to claim their earned rewards or unstake their deposits, leading to a loss of funds.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_solvency() public view { uint256 totalAllocated = 0; for(uint i=0; i<setServiceIds.length; i++) { totalAllocated += mapServiceInfo[setServiceIds[i]].reward; } assert(availableRewards + totalAllocated <= balance); }

invariants.description: A checkpoint cannot be executed in the same block as a service donation to prevent flash loan manipulation.
invariants.attack_scenario: An attacker uses a flash loan to donate a massive amount of ETH to a service, artificially inflating the Inverse Discount Factor (IDF) and extracting outsized OLAS rewards in the same block before repaying the loan.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_flash_loan_protection() public view { assert(lastDonationBlockNumber < block.number || !isCheckpointing); }

invariants.description: The last claimed epoch for a staking target must be monotonically increasing to prevent double-claiming of staking incentives.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the last claimed epoch can be reset or does not strictly increase, an attacker could repeatedly claim OLAS staking incentives for the same historical epochs, draining the dispenser treasury.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_claim_monotonicity(bytes32 nomineeHash) public view { assert(mapLastClaimedStakingEpochs[nomineeHash] >= previousClaimedEpochs[nomineeHash]); }

invariants.description: A user's total allocated voting power across all nominees cannot exceed the maximum allowed weight (100% or 10,000 bps).
invariants.attack_scenario: If a user can allocate more than 100% of their veOLAS voting power, they can artificially inflate the emission weights for multiple staking gauges, stealing inflation rewards from honest gauges.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_max_voting_power(address user) public view { assert(voteUserPower[user] <= 10000); }

invariants.description: A service cannot be unstaked before the minimum staking duration has elapsed, unless the contract has run out of rewards.
invariants.attack_scenario: An attacker stakes a service right before a checkpoint, claims rewards, and immediately unstakes, extracting yield without providing the required long-term liveness and security to the network.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_min_staking_duration(uint256 serviceId) public view { if(isUnstaking[serviceId]) { assert(block.timestamp - mapServiceInfo[serviceId].tsStart > minStakingDuration || availableRewards == 0); } }

invariants.description: The effective bond amount must correctly track the unused OLAS inflation allocated for bonding, conserving the protocol's liability.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the effective bond is inflated or under-accounted, the protocol might mint more OLAS than the inflation schedule permits, or unfairly deny legitimate bonding programs from being created.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_effective_bond_conservation() public view { assert(effectiveBond == historicalEffectiveBond + historicalMaxBond - historicalReserved + historicalRefunded); }
